design_philosophy.core_loop: Deploy units on grid-based map → position for advantage → execute turn-based combat → manage resources (ammo, fuel, terrain) → achieve objective (destroy enemy or capture HQ)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Asymmetrical positioning: terrain provides defense bonuses; unit variety requires tactical counter-selection; limited ammunition forces resource conservation decisions
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command an army as a strategic mastermind, orchestrating coordinated unit movements and exploiting terrain advantages to achieve victory against intelligent opponents
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Turn-based grid strategy with deterministic mechanics (no RNG on damage calculations) emphasizes planning over reflexes; faction-based CO system adds asymmetrical playstyles; terrain as primary tactical layer rather than cosmetic

difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based progression with replay capability for rank improvement
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can replay battles to achieve higher rank ratings (speed/power/technique); War Room mode provides graduated map difficulty for score optimization

progression.upgrade_system: Ranking system (Rank #100 to #1) and coin currency earned through campaign and War Room modes to unlock Commanding Officers and battle maps
progression.unlock_mechanism: Battle completion grants rank advancement and coins; additional rank bonus awarded for effort metrics (speed, power, technique)
replayability_hooks: Rank system incentivizes replaying maps for higher scores
War Room mode offers collection of maps for high-score competition
Map design mode for custom content creation

world_building_techniques: Campaign mode carries main storyline with faction context (Orange Star, Blue Moon, Yellow Comet, Green Earth nations)
Commanding officers represent faction identities and strategic personalities

danger_signals: Worldwide war triggered by false accusations of unprovoked attacks
environmental_storytelling: Terrain types (ports, airports, bases, HQ) indicate military infrastructure and strategic value
Captured cities and properties reflect territorial control and faction presence

sources_used: wikipedia - Advance Wars (used for Wars series context and gameplay mechanics; Super Famicom Wars is predecessor to Advance Wars with similar system design)
